label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking which statement is NOT true based on the text about Bobby Moresco. First, I'll go through each option one by one. Option A: "His father did not support his work as a bartender." Wait, the text says that when Moresco moved to Hollywood, he drove a cab and worked as a waiter. His father told him to "Stop this craziness and get a job..." So the father was against his acting career, not specifically the job as a waiter or bartender. The text doesn't mention a bartender; it mentions a waiter. But the key here is whether the father supported or not supported his work in those jobs. The father's concern was about the acting career, not the jobs he had to support himself. So this statement might be incorrect because the father didn't necessarily not support the waiter job, he was against the acting career. So A might be the false statement. Option B: "Before he became an overnight success, his life experienced ups and downs." The text describes a lot of struggles, moving to Hollywood, working odd jobs, financial difficulties, etc. So B is true. Option C: "His brother's death inspired his writing Half-Deserted Streets." The text says that after his brother Thomas was murdered, he started writing about the pain of Hell's Kitchen and the play was based on his brother's killing. So C is true. Option D: "Moresco grew up in New York's Hell's Kitchen which is a few blocks east of Broadway." The text states that Hell's Kitchen lies next to Broadway and that he needed to walk a few blocks east to get to Broadway. So Hell's Kitchen is next to Broadway but the direction is a bit tricky. If Hell's Kitchen is next to Broadway, then the statement says it's a few blocks east of Broadway. But if the neighborhood is next to Broadway, it might not be east of it.
